findings.title: ADR-016 packs post-claim Git closure extraction into spec §11.6's `spawn_grace_s` cold-path watchdog, and fences `run.launch-bind` on it — undeclared deviation
findings.claim: ADR-016 Decision 3 branch 3 bounds pipeline setup by "min(15 minutes, remaining first-heartbeat spawn_grace_s)" and Decision 6 fences the launch receipt: "requires lock-domain `now` to be strictly before both `acquired_at + spawn_grace_s` and the hard deadline". Decision 6's ordering makes the whole chain "persistent-own-folder validation → trace/attachment reconciliation → ephemeral launch projections → setup → gateway/guard → agent create → complete host trust recheck → inspect → pipeline `run.launch-bind` receipt → start" fit inside that window. But ADR-017 Decision 5 puts a full containerized Git object-closure extraction inside `setup`: "Fixed plumbing walks the complete object closure reachable from the pinned commit and streams it into a new task-local pack... The helper verifies every object hash/type", plus index build, tree materialization, and "A final `fsck`/tree/index/worktree comparison must be clean" — all over VirtioFS binds on Docker Desktop, alongside ADR-019's guard bootstrap and a Playwright-bearing agent image create. At the shipped default this is a 60-second budget, so the ADR's own 15-minute arm is dead code for the pipeline tier, and every overrun is charged: ADR-016 Decision 6 states the failure "is charged only by the ordinary spawn-grace reaper on a later tick". No IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES entry declares this; the two logged entries for this range cover task-local Git *location* and stale-writer cleanup ordering, neither of which mentions spawn_grace or the setup budget.
findings.evidence:
ADR-016 Decision 3: "Pipeline setup is bounded by
   `min(15 minutes, remaining first-heartbeat spawn_grace_s)` and must abandon
   before that grace expires". ADR-016 Decision 6: "requires lock-domain `now` to be strictly before both
`acquired_at + spawn_grace_s` and the hard deadline, then stores the id/time." — CONTRADICTED BY spec §11.6: "`spawn_grace_s` absorbs the container cold path — image pull, proxy wiring, `docker run` startup — and a lock slot that was stamped but never produced a first heartbeat is a failed container spawn, reaped in seconds." (no repository work is contemplated inside it); spec §16.3 Timing table: "`heartbeat_interval_s` / `spawn_grace_s` | 60 / 60"; spec §10: "reap: mark the run reaped, decrement the subject's `dispatch_retries`... At `dispatch_retries = 0`, set `blocked` with the reason instead" and §16.3 budgets: "`dispatch_retries` (per task, infra-death budget) | 3". Net effect on the stated macOS/Docker-Desktop/VirtioFS target: a first standalone Worker on any nontrivial repository exhausts its 3-retry infra budget and blocks the task, with no deviation logged (AGENTS.md §6).

findings.title: ADR-017 converts spec §11.3's RW `/workspace` scratch root into an RO mode-0555 bind for standalone-task roles — undeclared deviation
findings.claim: ADR-017 Decision 6's destination table makes `/workspace` a read-only bind for every standalone-task role, and Decision 5 makes that deliberate and non-recoverable: the parent is 0555 so the agent cannot create anything beside the two precreated children. Spec §11.3's mount table pins `/workspace` as RW container-local scratch whose stated purpose is "staged outputs, evidence captures". Packager and Refiner are left with no writable location anywhere under `/workspace` except the nested artifact children (ADR-017 Decision 6 gives them `/workspace/source` and `/workspace/git` "inherited through the RO task-root bind"). The logged deviation "Standalone tasks use a sanitized task-local Git repository" scopes itself to "spec §5, §6.2, §7, §11.1" and its Choice text only changes where the branch/worktree lives — it never mentions §11.3's `/workspace` row or the loss of the RW scratch root. Per AGENTS.md §6 the omission is the finding.
findings.evidence: ADR-017 Decision 6: "| `/workspace` | exact operator-owned mode-0555 task-local skeleton root, always RO; image-rootfs directory otherwise | standalone-task roles use the bind; every other agent gets only the structural rootfs directory |" and Decision 5: "The task root has one registered identity, is operator-owned mode 0555, and contains only the two resident-precreated children plus fixed structural mount points... Thus uid 10002 cannot chmod the parent or create a sibling" — CONTRADICTED BY spec §11.3 mount table: "| `/workspace` | container-local scratch | RW | staged outputs, evidence captures — dies with the container; anything durable leaves through the artifact roots or `mc` |". IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md entry "2026-07-13 — Standalone tasks use a sanitized task-local Git repository" declares "Where: Phase-3 committed-state mount review; spec §5, §6.2, §7, §11.1" — §11.3 and the RW→RO change of `/workspace` are absent from every entry in the file.

findings.title: ADR-017 mandates an initiative/child refusal that ADR-016's closed consequence table cannot express
findings.claim: ADR-017 requires an initiative/wave-child candidate to be refused as unsupported, but ADR-016 Decision 4 declares its consequence classes closed and enumerates every stable code — none of which names an unsupported/parked candidate shape, and ADR-017's own "Stable rejection codes" block has no such code either. A wave child selected by spec §10's query (3) (`seeded`, task → Worker) against a Git-backed Worksource therefore has no representable refusal: ADR-016 Decision 3 states "It never falls through to another candidate", so the candidate either loops on `preflight.stale` every tick (no mutation, no other work selected — a liveness wedge at the pipeline tier) or is blocked under a mount code that misdescribes it. The existing note "A promoted operator initiative dead-ends while the wave verb is parked" covers Editor promotion and the zero-wave arc packet, not this refusal path.
findings.evidence: ADR-017 Decision 5: "This ADR accepts no initiative mutable-worktree or Git-control arm; an initiative/child candidate needing that path is not eligible for the accepted Phase-3 spawn path until the operator resolves the parked design" and acceptance: "An initiative/child candidate that needs the parked shared worktree is refused as unsupported rather than receiving a standalone worktree, committed projection, or live primary fallback." — vs ADR-016 Decision 4: "The v1 consequence classes are closed:" whose Candidate-policy row is exactly "ADR-017's exact `mount.*` set, except the two deployment allowlist-trust codes above... `env.invalid`, `env.forbidden`; `auth.binding_invalid`, `auth.delivery_invalid`, `auth.ca_binding_mismatch`; `network.rule_invalid`, `network.rule_unresolved`, `network.destination_forbidden`, `network.policy_unappliable`, `network.policy_mismatch`; `identity.name_invalid`", and ADR-017's "Stable rejection codes" list (`mount.allowlist_untrusted` … `mount.runtime_unappliable`) contains no unsupported-shape code.

findings.title: The `mc-probe-mount-<p12>` canary's network mode is unpinned across all four ADRs while ADR-019 forbids Docker's default bridge and its acceptance inspects network mode on all five probes
findings.claim: ADR-018 Decision 8 pins `NetworkMode=container:<probe-guard-id>` for the two started clients and ADR-019 pins `none` for the `setup`-class Git canary, but neither ADR states a network mode for the never-started mount canary. ADR-019 assigns it the candidate's `pipeline`/`homie` tuple, whose Decision-3 network mode is `container:<their-guard>` — a guard that does not exist at preclaim (only `mc-probe-guard-<p12>` does). A `docker create` with no NetworkMode assigns the default bridge, which ADR-019 explicitly prohibits, and ADR-019's acceptance nevertheless demands an inspected exact network mode for all five probe containers. The result is an acceptance row that cannot be written because no ADR fixes the value.
findings.evidence: ADR-019 Decision 3: "Network mode is exact: pipeline/Homie join `container:<their-guard>`; helper/setup/landing use `none`; only the guard joins its isolated routed bridge. No class silently receives Docker's default bridge." and acceptance: "ADR-018's preclaim objects are not an exception: ... both started capless clients and the never-started mount canary use the selected candidate's `pipeline` or `homie` tuple... Tests inspect every hard/resource/security field on all five containers" plus "Docker inspect proves exact `NanoCpus`, `Memory`, `MemorySwap`, `PidsLimit`, user, privilege, `CapDrop`, `CapAdd`, security options, network mode, namespaces" — but ADR-018 Decision 8 pins network only for the clients ("Each network client uses `NetworkMode=container:<probe-guard-id>`") and ADR-016 Decision 5 describes the canary only as "one **unstarted** `mc-probe-mount-<p12>` canary container from the candidate image with the candidate's exact existing structural bind/volume set and access flags" — no network field anywhere in 016/017/018/019.

findings.title: Ledger's "two explicit spec deviations" undercounts what 20a1a50 actually logged (both claimed entries verified present)
findings.claim: Both entries the ledger names exist in IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md and are well-formed. But commit 20a1a50 appended eight entries, at least four of which are spec deviations in the AGENTS.md §6 sense (they carry a "Spec impact:" line proposing spec text changes), not merely informational. Anyone auditing the range from the ledger alone would look for two and miss four more that are load-bearing for Phase 3 — notably the `open+audit` control-address floor, which weakens §11.4's literal text. This is a ledger-accuracy issue only; no ADR content is at fault.
findings.evidence: PROGRESS.md (20a1a50): "The two explicit spec deviations—pre-landing task-local Git and one stale-writer cleanup before Console/landing—are appended to `IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md`" — vs `git show 20a1a50 -- IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md` which adds eight `##` entries: "`open+audit` retains a control-address floor" (Spec impact: "§11.4 should replace 'everything' with 'everything outside the non-removable control-address floor…'"), "Homie trace projection supersedes individual file mounts" (Spec impact: "§15.3 should name a bounded same-inode historical-trace view"), "Helper uses a component label, not an agent tier" (Spec impact: "§11.5 should replace 'mc-tier' for the helper with the component label"), "Shared trace projection contains pipeline traces only", "A null-locator Homie preflight refusal is non-terminal", "Explicit row resume supersedes null-locator refusal suppression", plus the two named ones.

summary: Reviewed ADRs 016–019 (commit 20a1a50) against specs/mission-control-spec.md, docs/phase3-contract.md, PROGRESS.md "## Parked", and IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md. The four ADRs are internally consistent on the axes I probed hardest: the guard's five-name bootstrap capability set, uid taxonomy (10001 setuid / 10002 agent / 10003 guard), label/tier taxonomy, deterministic name derivation, plan/collection bounds (256 mounts, 128 network rules), and the launch-nonce-vs-registration-generation split all agree across 016/017/018/019 and with the contract. ADR-016 correctly keeps ADR-018's secret launch nonce out of the plan while carrying the non-secret `mc-gateway-registration` generation as a label, and both sides agree on resident-restart adoption refusal. Contract coverage is good: every mechanism the contract's §1 delegation list names has an ADR behind it, and the parked initiative-wave/holistic-review question is explicitly refused rather than routed around (ADR-017 Decision 5). Both deviations the ledger claims for this range exist and are well-formed. Five findings: two major (an undeclared deviation that packs ADR-017's Git object-closure extraction into spec §11.6's 60s `spawn_grace_s` cold-path watchdog and fences `run.launch-bind` on it — which burns `dispatch_retries` and blocks first tasks on real repos; and an undeclared RW→RO change to spec §11.3's `/workspace` scratch row), two minor (ADR-017 mandates an initiative/child refusal with no code in ADR-016's closed consequence table; the never-started mount canary's network mode is unpinned though ADR-019 forbids the default bridge and inspects network mode on all five probes), and one info on ledger accuracy.

findings.title: ADR-016 gates the lease-free Homie tier behind the pipeline lease, breaking Inv. 1, §11.6 and §15.5
findings.claim: Decision 3's branch 1 makes every Homie action — wake, idle-end, exit reconciliation — unreachable whenever a pipeline run holds a fresh lease. Because the lease runs to `timeout_minutes`/`grace_minutes` (60/15) and the hard deadline, an operator's first message to a new or idled-out Homie can wait up to ~75 minutes instead of one tick, and an idle Homie container cannot be ended for the same window. This inverts the spec's stated direction of protection: the spec is explicit that a conversation must never wedge the pipeline AND that the pipeline's lease must never govern the Homie tier.
findings.evidence:
ADR-016 Decision 3, branch 1: "Only a temporally and envelope-fresh held lease returns `idle(lease-held)` immediately. No recovered diagnostic, Homie transition, landing, wake, or second cleanup action passes that “Fresh → return.”" — reinforced in Alternatives rejected: "**Wake Homie under a fresh pipeline lease.** Conflicts with §10's explicit `Fresh → return`; lease-free describes Homie's runtime liveness, not an exemption from the single dispatch order."

Contradicting spec text:
- Inv. 1 (§2): "Homie sessions are the explicit lease-free operator tier (§15), so they never count as pipeline runs."
- §11.1: "The one lease-free tier — a Homie operator session (§15) — **may legitimately run alongside it**, which is why containers carry a tier label and the lease is never the liveness test for Homie sessions (§11.6)."
- §11.6: "A `homie` container is swept against the Homie session registry (`homie_sessions`, §15.4) and its `homie_idle_timeout`, **never against the lease it deliberately does not hold**. … the tier label keeps the lease-keyed rule from reaping the one lease-free tier the spec itself authorizes (§15)." ADR-016 branch 6 ("Idle Homie end") is unreachable behind branch 1's `Fresh → return`, i.e. the idle sweep IS gated on the lease.
- §15.5: "The inbound commit makes the conversation eligible to run — the resident's next timer tick wakes a Homie container if one is not already warm (§10) … so **the tick latency is paid only on a session's first message or after an idle-out**." One tick, not one lease.
- §15.3: "a conversation can last an hour, and holding the global lease would freeze all real work" — the ADR achieves the same freeze in reverse.

The §10 sentences the ADR leans on ("(0) Reconcile the lease. Fresh → return"; "Dispatch runs only when the lease is free") sit inside §10's *pipeline* control table, which contains no Homie branch at all; §15 and §11.6 are the sections that actually govern the Homie tier, and they are the more specific text. Per AGENTS.md §6, the conservative option is the one that "preserves the 26 invariants" — this resolution does not preserve Inv. 1's lease-free tier.

findings.title: ADR-017's mode-0700 setuid-owned seal and attachment roots have no creation mechanism on the macOS target
findings.claim: Decision 4/5/6/8 require several `MC_HOME` roots to be *owned by uid 10001* (the setuid uid) at mode 0700, while other `MC_HOME` roots on the same VirtioFS share must be writable by *uid 10002* — and every one of these roots is precreated by the resident, which §12 pins as an unprivileged per-user LaunchAgent running as the operator's macOS uid. A non-root macOS process cannot `chown` a directory to uid 10001 or 10002, and the ADR names no mechanism (no privileged helper, no in-container chown step, no `install.sh` root phase) that performs it. As written the confinement property is unachievable, and because ADR-017 declares the canary red arm fatal ("A red arm refuses mutable dispatch; ownership/mode widening is not a fallback"), this fails closed onto a permanently dead Phase-3 mutable-task and attachment line rather than a repairable diagnostic.
findings.evidence:
Roots required to be 10001-owned/0700:
- ADR-017 Decision 4: "the current standalone Worker's exact completion-seal staging root: Docker RW but **privileged-owned mode 0700**, usable only by the setuid completion wrapper; uid 10002 has no direct traversal/write authority"
- ADR-017 Decision 4: "own outbound publisher root: Docker RW but **mode-0700/setuid-owned** so uid 10002 direct access is denied"
- ADR-017 Decision 8: "The Docker bind at `/mc/attachments/out` is RW for the privileged publisher but **its source root and parents are owned by the setuid uid at mode 0700**, so model/harness uid 10002 cannot traverse, create, overwrite, or delete there."

Roots on the same share required to be 10002-writable, precreated host-side:
- ADR-017 Decision 6 table: "`/mc/session` | `MC_HOME/sessions/<run-or-session-id>/`, RW"; "`/mc/records/output` | `MC_HOME/outputs/<run-id>/`, RW"; "`/home/agent` | `MC_HOME/state/worksources/<scope-id>/home`, RW synthetic home" — and "An expected-absent workflow/correction/output source **is precreated or mkdir'd by the resident** from the immutable effect"; Decision 5: "**The resident first creates and identity-registers** the empty `task-<id>/{source,git}` skeleton **as the host operator**, then fixes the parent 0555 and the children to the canary-proved final-uid writable shape."

Who the resident is — spec §12: "On macOS both are generated as ordinary **per-user LaunchAgents in the operator's GUI session, never LaunchDaemons**"; Inv. 23: "The resident process … and the dashboard are **ordinary host processes** under the host init system, not containers." AGENTS.md environment facts: "launchd loading during development: **never**" — i.e. no privileged install path is available to close this either.

The only proven prior for the ownership boundary is spike S1, whose chown runs against a **named volume in the Linux VM**, not a macOS bind: `spikes/01-setuid-gate/check.sh:51`: "chown $MCPRIV_UID:$MCPRIV_UID /spine && chmod 0700 /spine". ADR-017 imports that property onto the `MC_HOME` VirtioFS share ("the same-share VirtioFS capability canary") without carrying over a mechanism that can establish it there. `docs/phase3-contract.md` §3 lists "Final-uid VirtioFS and cgroup v1/v2-or-capability canaries are mandatory" — i.e. the behavior is unproven, and no ADR text supplies the chown.

findings.title: ADR-017 Decision 8 requires the host-native surface to read a tree it simultaneously makes unreadable to the host uid
findings.claim: Decision 8 states two requirements about the same `MC_HOME/attachments/<session-id>/out` subtree that cannot both hold: (a) the root *and its parents* are owned by uid 10001 at mode 0700 so uid 10002 cannot traverse, and (b) the already-running host-native surface — a process running as the operator's macOS uid — opens the published outbound object directly. Under any uid-faithful view of the share, a 0700 directory owned by uid 10001 denies traversal to the operator uid, so the mandatory canary's outbound arm cannot pass. The same contradiction appears for the completion seal, whose canary requires the host to verify a seal under a privileged-owned 0700 root. Decision 8 declares no fallback ("there is no fallback that changes ownership, widens modes/ACLs…"), so the contradiction resolves as a permanent red canary rather than a design.
findings.evidence:
ADR-017 Decision 8, ownership: "The Docker bind at `/mc/attachments/out` is RW for the privileged publisher but **its source root and parents are owned by the setuid uid at mode 0700**, so model/harness uid 10002 cannot traverse, create, overwrite, or delete there."

ADR-017 Decision 8, mandatory canary — the contradicting requirement: "setuid euid/fsuid 10001 then publishes a new outbound object and the **already-running host-native surface opens it**. … The canary restarts the container and **reopens both old objects** to prove persistence. Failure leaves attachment/Homie confinement unhealthy and refuses launch; **there is no fallback that changes ownership, widens modes/ACLs**, gives uid 10002 writable staging, or transports bytes through the spine."

ADR-017 Decision 8 also requires host-side reads at the reference layer: "Every referenced leaf and ancestor is revalidated non-symlink; the leaf must be a regular file whose identity, size, and digest match **before runner/native open**."

Same shape for the seal — ADR-017 Decision 5: "Mutable-task confinement is healthy only after the same-share VirtioFS capability canary proves, with mounts live, that euid/fsuid 10001 can publish and fsync a seal, uid 10002 gets `EACCES`, and a restarted setup container can read that seal RO **while the host verifies its identity/digest**" — against Decision 6: "`/mc/completion-seal` | exact `MC_HOME/seals/<run-id>/`, Docker RW but **privileged-owned mode 0700**".

Contradicting spec text — §15.5: "When Homie returns an image, its runner ensures the bytes land on the file plane and the reply row references them; **the native surface reads the file directly** and pushes it over its websocket. The bytes never enter the spine…" — and §12/Inv. 23 fix that native surface as an unprivileged per-user LaunchAgent, so "the host" here is always the operator uid, never uid 10001.

findings.title: ADR-016 forbids the helper the MC_HOME bind that §16.4 requires it to hold for backups, and ADR-019 has no class for a separate backup container
findings.claim: ADR-016 Decision 1 states the helper mounts nothing from `MC_HOME`, then says `mc backup` "may separately receive" its backup-target bind. §16.4 requires the opposite: the backup is a `VACUUM INTO` onto *the helper's* bind-mounted `MC_HOME/backups/` path, and a warm long-lived helper cannot acquire a bind per-verb. If the ADR instead means a separate short-lived backup container, ADR-019 closes the class list at six and has no `backup` class, while docs/phase3-contract.md requires finite resource bounds on every managed container — leaving the backup container unbounded or undispatchable.
findings.evidence:
ADR-016 Decision 1: "The helper mounts the spine named volume and **nothing from `MC_HOME`** or the host credential/file plane. `mc backup` may separately receive only its fixed backup-target bind."

Spec §16.4: "The resident's tick loop … past `backup_interval_s`, invokes `mc backup` **through the warm helper: a `VACUUM INTO` onto the helper's bind-mounted `MC_HOME/backups/` path**, written to a temp name and renamed on completion." (§16.1 likewise places `backups/` under `MC_HOME`: "its snapshots land under `MC_HOME/backups/`".)

ADR-019 Decision 1: "The same fields may appear for **exactly six** deployment-level classes" — the table lists `pipeline`, `homie`, `helper`, `network-guard`, `setup`, `landing`; no backup class. ADR-019 Decision 4: "an explicit invalid/unbounded tuple is configuration health: no claim/container". docs/phase3-contract.md §3, Resource bounds row: "Docker inspect proves finite CPU, memory, and PID bounds… All five preclaim probe containers are bounded too" — the enumeration has no backup entry either.

findings.title: ADR-017 makes /workspace read-only for standalone-task roles, contradicting §11.3 without declaring the deviation
findings.claim: Decision 6 binds `/workspace` as an operator-owned mode-0555 task-root, always RO, for every standalone-task role (Worker, Verifier, Packager, Refiner). §11.3's mount table fixes `/workspace` as RW container-local scratch whose stated purpose is staged outputs and evidence captures — the very artifacts §7's Verifier ladder produces (Playwright screenshots/video). ADR-017 enumerates its deviations as "§§6.2, 7, and 11.1" and does not name §11.3's `/workspace` row, so the change is unlogged; nothing in the ADR restates where §11.3's scratch semantics land.
findings.evidence:
ADR-017 Decision 6 table: "| `/workspace` | **exact operator-owned mode-0555 task-local skeleton root, always RO**; image-rootfs directory otherwise | standalone-task roles use the bind; every other agent gets only the structural rootfs directory |" — and Decision 5: "The task root has one registered identity, is **operator-owned mode 0555** … Thus uid 10002 cannot chmod the parent or create a sibling".

Contradicting spec §11.3 mount table: "| `/workspace` | container-local scratch | **RW** | staged outputs, evidence captures — dies with the container; anything durable leaves through the artifact roots or `mc` |".

Relevant consumer — §7 Promotion Gate Ladder: "The Verifier owns the ladder — staging deploy, the Playwright criterion walk, the security/dependency scan, **recording each gate's evidence**"; "**coding:** … staging deploy + Playwright walk (screenshots/video)".

ADR-017's own deviation declaration is narrower than the change: "This topology is an explicit, conservative deviation from **§§6.2, 7, and 11.1**: before approval, `mc/task-<id>` exists only in the task-local repository rather than the real Worksource repository." §11.3 is not named there, and AGENTS.md §6 requires deviations to land in IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md.

findings.title: Inv. 26 / trace-projection resolution verified: it holds
findings.claim: The ledger's claim that the "Inv. 26 tension" was "resolved conservatively" checks out rather than merely being asserted. §15.3 independently grants the operator-scope Homie read-only mounts across session files, so Decision 7's owner-keyed same-inode hardlink projection is strictly *narrower* than the spec's own grant on three axes (finalized-only, pipeline-only, no folder mount), and Decision 8's attachment plane sits outside `sessions/` entirely, leaving the trace-only property literal. No finding — recorded so the check is not repeated.
findings.evidence:
Spec §15.3 Confinement (the authorizing grant the ADR relies on): "Homies run containerized like everything else, under a dedicated **operator-scope profile: read-only mounts across all Worksources' workspaces, session files, and artifacts**, plus board-wide reads via `mc get/list`. This is one of two declared read-only exceptions to Inv. 22's per-Worksource scoping".

Inv. 26's literal requirement is preserved — ADR-017 Decision 7: "A run's own `sessions/<run_id>/` directory is mounted RW only into that run … **The original `sessions/<id>/` folder is never mounted, and no trace entry is a Docker mount.**" and "**Homie-owned native traces are never linked into this shared projection** … Ended Homie traces are excluded because resume can append to their inode".

Inv. 26 text checked against: "Each run … owns one trace-only session folder on the file plane, created before its container starts and **mounted only into that container** … Session folders are never swept". §11.3: "Each container mounts **only its own** folder". Both are statements about the folder mount, which the projection does not perform; the residual visibility of finalized pipeline trace bytes to Homie is exactly what §15.3 authorizes. Decision 8 keeps attachments out of the invariant's scope: "Neither direction lives under `sessions/`, so the trace-only invariant remains literal." Consistent with IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md:683-699.

summary:
Reviewed all four Phase-3 ADRs (016, 017, 018, 019) against the 26 invariants in specs/mission-control-spec.md §2 and the sections they delegate from (§§5, 6.2, 7, 9.1, 10, 11.1–11.7, 12, 15.3–15.5, 16.1–16.4). Six findings: three major, two minor, one info.

The three majors cluster on two root causes.

(1) Lease-gating of the lease-free tier. ADR-016 Decision 3's branch 1 lets a fresh pipeline lease return `idle(lease-held)` before any Homie branch runs, and its Alternatives-rejected section makes this deliberate. That contradicts Inv. 1, §11.1, §11.6's "never against the lease it deliberately does not hold," and §15.5's one-tick wake latency. The ADR leans on §10's "Fresh → return" — but that sentence lives inside §10's pipeline control table, which has no Homie branch; §15/§11.6 are the specific text governing that tier. The cost is up to ~75 minutes of no Homie wake, no idle-end, and no exit reconciliation.

(2) The setuid ownership boundary is imported from a named volume onto the macOS VirtioFS `MC_HOME` share with no mechanism behind it. ADR-017 requires `MC_HOME/seals/<run-id>/` and `MC_HOME/attachments/<sid>/out` to be uid-10001-owned at 0700 while `sessions/`, `outputs/`, and `state/.../home` on the same share stay uid-10002-writable — all precreated by a resident that §12 pins as an unprivileged per-user LaunchAgent that cannot chown to either uid. Worse, Decision 8 requires the operator-uid host-native surface to open published objects *inside* the 0700/10001 tree its own canary must prove uid 10002 cannot traverse; §15.5 ("the native surface reads the file directly") makes that host read mandatory. Both arms of the mandatory canary cannot pass, and both ADRs forbid a fallback — so this fails closed onto a permanently dead mutable-task and attachment line rather than a repairable diagnostic. The only prior evidence for the boundary (spike S1) chowns a Linux-VM named volume, not a bind; phase3-contract.md still lists the final-uid VirtioFS canary as unproven.

Minors: ADR-016 forbids the helper the `MC_HOME/backups/` bind that §16.4 requires it to hold (and ADR-019's six-class table has no class for a separate backup container); ADR-017 makes `/workspace` RO for standalone-task roles against §11.3's RW-scratch row without declaring that deviation alongside its §§6.2/7/11.1 one.

On the specific item the ledger flagged: the Inv. 26 resolution genuinely holds. §15.3 independently grants Homie read-only mounts across session files, so Decision 7's hardlink projection is strictly narrower than the spec's own grant, and Decision 8's attachment plane sits outside `sessions/`. Recorded as info so the check isn't repeated.

Also checked and found clean, so not reported: ADR-018's `open+audit` control-address floor (a tightening, and properly logged at IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md:701); the frozen-binding Homie wake under a malformed `routing.md` (resume never consults `routing.md` per §15.3/§15.4, and config is still attested); orphan cleanup ahead of Console/landing (sanctioned by §11.6's "runs at every tick start"); ADR-018 Decision 9's guard-death raw-egress window (declared, tested, and no authority expansion beyond already-granted `network_allow` tuples); ADR-019's NNP split (setup/landing are not agent containers per §11.5); and prepare/attest/commit against Inv. 2 (commit re-decides under `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`, so single-writer and CAS claim hold).

No files were modified; no Docker, launchd, or OPERATOR-INPUTS.md access.

findings.title: ADR-018 asserts separate user namespaces, which would break its own `meta skuid` enforcement and contradicts the pinned target
findings.claim: ADR-018's central enforcement mechanism is per-uid nftables filtering across containers that share one network namespace, but the ADR twice states that the containers' user namespaces are separate. On the actual target that is false — and if it were true, the mechanism could not work. The precondition it silently depends on (no userns remap / Enhanced Container Isolation off) never appears in Decision 8's exhaustive failure-class list.
findings.evidence: ADR-018 Decision 1: "The agent joins its network namespace with `NetworkMode=container:<guard-id>` and has no independent endpoint. PID, mount, user, IPC, and capability namespaces remain separate." Repeated in Decision 8: "shares only the guard's network namespace; PID, mount, user, IPC, and cgroup membership remain separate." The enforcement rests on: "every owner predicate is an nftables `meta skuid` match against the socket's kernel credential" separating "uid 10003 (the live guard)", "uid 10002 (agent/runner/harness/tools)", and "euid 10001 (setuid `mc`)". `meta skuid` compares kernel uids; the guard installs its ruleset from the init user namespace, so it can only distinguish 10001/10002/10003 because Docker Desktop puts every container in a SHARED init user namespace with an identity map. The trusted spike evidence says exactly that: spikes/01-setuid-gate/RESULT.md assertion 8 — "Canary: no user-namespace remap (daemon SecurityOptions + identity `/proc/self/uid_map` `0 0 4294967295` → Enhanced Container Isolation off) | PASS". specs/implementation-handoff.md line 458 pins it as an operator obligation: "Enhanced Container Isolation **off**, no user-namespace remap". specs/mission-control-spec.md §11.7 (line 554) names the failure mode: "A probe failure refuses fail-closed with a message naming the likely causes: `no-new-privileges` forced, uid remapping (rootless mode), or Docker Desktop's Enhanced Container Isolation (which must stay off)." ADR-018 Decision 8 claims "Failure classes are exact" and enumerates "missing nf_tables or inability to create any guard; ... no cgroup/network capability" as deployment health — but never uid remap/ECI, even though a remapped daemon would silently make every `meta skuid` predicate stop matching (fail-open on the owner predicates, not fail-closed, since the default-drop chain would still deny but the guard's own uid-10003 exception would also fail, wedging the guard rather than proving the separation the ADR's acceptance tests assert).

findings.title: ADR-019's per-class ranges have no aggregate budget check; a fully in-range config exceeds the pinned VM and is classified as infrastructure failure, not config health
findings.claim: Decision 1's "Allowed operator range" is validated field-by-field per class only. The sum of maxima for classes the spec explicitly permits to be live concurrently is ~13 GiB against a pinned ≥8 GB VM, and ADR-019 deliberately rejects any capacity-aware validation. The resulting OOM is then classified as infrastructure failure — an unbounded failure loop with no configuration-health signal, which is precisely the class Decision 4 reserves for config problems.
findings.evidence: ADR-019 Decision 1 ranges: `pipeline` "1000–4000m / 2048–6144 MiB", `homie` "500–2000m / 1024–4096 MiB", `network-guard` "250–1000m / 256–1024 MiB", `helper` "250–1000m / 256–1024 MiB". A live pipeline agent + its guard + a live Homie + its guard + the helper at maxima = 6144+1024+4096+1024+1024 = 13,312 MiB. specs/mission-control-spec.md §11.1 explicitly permits that concurrency: "The one lease-free tier — a Homie operator session (§15) — may legitimately run alongside it, which is why containers carry a tier label". specs/implementation-handoff.md line 458 pins the machine: "≥4 CPU / ≥8 GB to the VM". ADR-019 Decision 4 only says "Unknown classes/keys, duplicate values, zero/negative values, `-1`, strings such as `unlimited|max|4g`, and out-of-range integers reject" — nothing checks the sum. ADR-019 Alternatives rejected: "Dynamic percentages of current VM capacity make plans non-replayable." Decision 2 then routes the consequence away from config health: "An OOM or other runtime-observable limit termination is infrastructure failure, never a quality verdict." Decision 4 states the intended posture the sum-check omission defeats: "Resource configuration is deployment-owned and has no profile override, so an explicit invalid/unbounded tuple is configuration health: no claim/container and no task charge/block."

findings.title: ADR-019 pins a 256-PID range floor for the class that must run Chromium, with no spike evidence that Playwright fits under a task-counting `pids.max`
findings.claim: ADR-019 makes 256 PIDs the *lowest allowed operator value* for `pipeline` and then requires the S8 Chromium/Playwright canary to pass at exactly that minimum. `pids.max` counts tasks (threads), not processes, and Chromium routinely exceeds 256 tasks. S8 — the trusted evidence the ADR cites — ran with no PID limit at all and records nothing about task counts, so the range floor of the ADR's own central table is undecided.
findings.evidence: ADR-019 Decision 1 table: `pipeline` | "2000m / 4096 MiB / 512" | "1000–4000m / 2048–6144 MiB / 256–1024". Decision 2: "`PidsLimit = pids`" and "on cgroup v2 this is finite `cpu.max`, `memory.max`, `memory.swap.max=0`, and `pids.max`". Acceptance: "Mechanism canaries run at the **minimum** allowed envelope: S8 Chromium/Playwright in pipeline, non-root with the spike-accepted `--no-sandbox` browser flag". spikes/08-arm64-image/RESULT.md records only "A6 | One Chromium launch + screenshot from inside the container (Playwright), as non-root `agent` user | PASS | Chromium 149.0.7827.0" and the sole browser constraint it found — "**Chromium as non-root needs `--no-sandbox`**"; the spike ran no PidsLimit and recorded no process/thread count, so it does not evidence 256. ADR-019 anticipates the symptom without pricing it into the range: "PID exhaustion may surface only as `fork/clone` `EAGAIN` and need not terminate the container". Its escape hatch — "If a minimum cannot pass its mechanism canary, the ADR minimum is raised; the test is never bypassed and the limit is never removed" — means the accepted range table must be re-opened, i.e. the decision this ADR exists to pin is not actually made.

findings.title: ADR-018's mandatory five-container preclaim probe makes a spawn tick outlast the spec's stated reap-latency bound
findings.claim: Every spawn/wake candidate must create, start, inspect, stop and remove five containers plus a bridge, inside a control channel whose base wall allowance is 120 seconds. The spec's tick is 60s and skips a firing that lands while a tick is in flight, so a spawn tick legitimately consumes 2+ tick intervals and defers the following tick's lease reconciliation. Neither ADR-018 nor ADR-016 restates or relaxes the spec's documented bound.
findings.evidence: ADR-018 Decision 8: "Every spawn/wake candidate gets exactly one temporary guard and two separate capless started clients, plus the never-started mount canary and the Git-setup canary below: five containers total." Decision 6 sets the budget: "Non-inventory work, including the Docker probe, remains within the base 120 seconds" (with "the one-use channel wall allowance is `120 seconds + 5 seconds * total_page_count`"). specs/mission-control-spec.md §16.2 tunables table: "| `tick_interval_s` (heartbeat-tick timer; reap-latency bounds are threshold + one tick) | 60 |". §10: "a plain in-process interval timer firing every `tick_interval_s` (60s default). One tick runs at a time — a firing that lands while a tick is in flight is skipped, never queued or run concurrently". A spawn tick that spends up to 120s in the probe therefore skips at least one subsequent tick, so reap latency after any spawn attempt is "threshold + up to three ticks", not the spec's "threshold + one tick". ADR-018 Decision 8 also makes the probe unskippable on a refused candidate — "A production candidate cannot skip this fixture merely because a read-only policy calculation succeeded" — so a repeatedly refused candidate pays the full cost every tick.

findings.title: ADR-019 calls the network guard "credential-free"; ADR-018 gives it a 32-byte runtime launch credential
findings.claim: ADR-019's Decision 1 characterises the `network-guard` class as holding no credential, directly contradicting ADR-018 Decision 6, which streams a 32-byte one-shot launch credential into the guard's mlocked memory over its stdin and makes every gateway connection present it. An implementer reading ADR-019's class definition as the guard's contract would omit the credential plane ADR-018's gateway authentication depends on.
findings.evidence: ADR-019 Decision 1: "The resident-hosted credential gateway is native (§12), so it has no Docker class here. `network-guard` is only ADR-018's credential-free runtime-side namespace/firewall/relay." ADR-018 Decision 1 contradicts it: the guard "receives a one-shot launch credential as described in Decision 6" and "It receives only its non-secret policy projection and resolver inputs plus the private one-shot launch credential." ADR-018 Decision 4: "Every connection also presents the launch credential before any proxy bytes are accepted." ADR-018 Decision 6: "The runtime credential is exactly 32 random bytes. The resident streams one bounded binary startup frame over the newly created guard's attached stdin into guard-private `mlock`ed memory before readiness; the guard sets itself non-dumpable and closes stdin after that frame."

findings.title: ADR-019 attributes its machine-budget premise to the spec, which contains no CPU/memory floor
findings.claim: ADR-019's "Where the spec delegates" header sources its ≥4 CPU/≥8 GiB premise to the spec. That number is not in the spec at all; it is an operator-input row in the handoff. Under AGENTS.md ("The spec wins on conflict") an ADR whose range table is anchored to a misattributed authority is a traceability defect.
findings.evidence: ADR-019 header: "Where the spec delegates: the Phase-3 handoff requires resource bounds; the spec fixes Docker Desktop ≥4 CPU/≥8 GiB, S1's setuid preconditions, and S8's browser image but supplies no per-container values or override grammar." `grep -niE "8 ?gi?b|4 ?cpu|memory|ram" specs/mission-control-spec.md` returns no CPU or memory floor; the spec's §16.2 [container] knob table lists only runtime, base image tag, package-cache root, mount allowlist path, and additional blocked patterns. The actual source is specs/implementation-handoff.md line 458: "Enhanced Container Isolation **off**, no user-namespace remap; VirtioFS backend noted; **Resource Saver disabled** ...; ≥4 CPU / ≥8 GB to the VM" — and it says ≥8 **GB**, not GiB.

summary:
Reviewed ADR-016/017/018/019 (commit 20a1a50) against specs/mission-control-spec.md, specs/implementation-handoff.md, docs/phase3-contract.md, IMPLEMENTATION-NOTES.md, and the trusted Phase-0 evidence in spikes/01-setuid-gate, spikes/04-egress-gateway, spikes/06-dispatch-table, spikes/08-arm64-image. Lens: implementability on macOS/Apple Silicon + Docker Desktop.

The ADR set is unusually thorough on the target's hard edges and I could not fault most of what the prompt flagged for scrutiny. Specifically, these hold up and are NOT findings: ADR-018's loopback-only native gateway reached via `host.docker.internal` (real Docker Desktop behavior, and the ADR makes it a permanent canary rather than assuming it); the CA/header-injection and WebSocket-MITM shape (matches S4 assertions 1-11 and S4 finding #1 on codex streaming over wss); raw-TCP-only `network_allow` with rule-granularity audit (matches spec §11.4's explicit "declared standing allowance" limitation and S4's `--internal`+relay evidence, which the ADR consciously supersedes with a reasoned Context paragraph); the bootstrap capability sequence (SETPCAP is present, so dropping NET_BIND_SERVICE from the bounding set is achievable; NNP does not block a root->10003 setuid); ADR-019's NNP-absent rows for spine-mounting classes (matches S1 assertion 9) and `--no-sandbox` (matches S8's note); ADR-017's `os.SameFile` walk and macOS ACL reads (these run host-side on APFS, not across VirtioFS, so the VirtioFS concern does not apply) and its claim that "Docker inspect verifies only canonical source spelling, destination, and mode; Docker does not expose host device/inode identity" (accurate); ADR-017's mandatory final-uid VirtioFS write canary before any mutable Worksource (the right answer to Docker Desktop's uid-translation uncertainty, with no widening fallback); ADR-016's spool-backed bounded fan-in inventory and 64-row-page O(tail) resume (both genuinely bounded). Nothing in the four ADRs requires fsnotify on a bind mount or a launchd load — ADR-017 Decision 7 explicitly pins polling ("Consumers enumerate by polling and never rely on fsnotify across the VirtioFS bind").

Six findings, one major. The major one is ADR-018's twice-repeated claim that the guard and agent keep separate user namespaces: that is false on the pinned target (S1 assertion 8; handoff line 458) and, if it were true, would defeat the `meta skuid` uid separation that is the ADR's entire enforcement plane — and the no-remap/ECI-off precondition is absent from Decision 8's "exact" deployment-health failure list even though nf_tables and cgroup capability are there. The rest are contained gaps: ADR-019 validates resource tuples per-class only, so an in-range config sums to ~13 GiB against the pinned ≥8 GB VM and lands in the infrastructure-failure class rather than config health; ADR-019's 256-PID range floor for the Chromium-bearing `pipeline` class is unevidenced by S8 (which ran no PidsLimit) while `pids.max` counts threads; ADR-018's mandatory five-container preclaim probe (120s base budget) makes a spawn tick outrun the spec's documented "threshold + one tick" reap bound at `tick_interval_s`=60; ADR-019 calls the guard "credential-free" while ADR-018 hands it a 32-byte mlocked launch credential; and ADR-019 misattributes its ≥4 CPU/≥8 GiB premise to the spec (it is a handoff operator-input row, and reads ≥8 GB).

Key files: /Users/vinchenkov/Documents/dev/ai/homie/docs/adr/018-network-guard-gateway.md, /Users/vinchenkov/Documents/dev/ai/homie/docs/adr/019-container-resource-envelopes.md, /Users/vinchenkov/Documents/dev/ai/homie/spikes/01-setuid-gate/RESULT.md, /Users/vinchenkov/Documents/dev/ai/homie/spikes/08-arm64-image/RESULT.md, /Users/vinchenkov/Documents/dev/ai/homie/specs/implementation-handoff.md (line 458), /Users/vinchenkov/Documents/dev/ai/homie/specs/mission-control-spec.md (§11.1, §11.4, §11.7 line 554, §16.2).
